understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to complete all levels of the KA59 puzzle by placing every selectable piece onto the goal tiles (marked +) simultaneously, using a finite number of steps per level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was in pixel coordinates on a grid where 3 pixels equal one cell. MOVE actions shifted the currently selected piece in the chosen direction. Some objects were pushable blocks that moved along with the selected piece when pushed. Walls and boundaries blocked movement, though their exact behavior varied and required experimentation. Multiple selectable pieces existed per level, and CLICK with a target_position in pixel coordinates switched control to another selectable piece at that location. The semantic_grid provided an ASCII overview of the level layout.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to complete all levels of the KA59 puzzle by placing every selectable piece onto the goal tiles (marked +) simultaneously, using a finite number of steps per level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was in pixel coordinates on a grid where 3 pixels equal one cell. MOVE actions shifted the currently selected piece in the chosen direction. Some objects were pushable blocks that moved along with the selected piece when pushed. Walls and boundaries blocked movement, though their exact behavior varied and required experimentation. Multiple selectable pieces existed per level, and CLICK with a target_position in pixel coordinates switched control to another selectable piece at that location. The semantic_grid provided an ASCII overview of the level layout.
